title: Golden hour photography playbook
text: Golden hour starts roughly 40 minutes before sunset at mid-latitudes but only 20 near the equator, where the sun drops fast — in Bali or the Maldives, be in position early. Meter for the highlights and let foregrounds go dark for silhouettes, or bracket three exposures for blends. After the sun drops, wait: the best color often lands 10–25 minutes later during civil twilight. Apps like PhotoPills or Sun Surveyor show exactly where on the horizon the sun will fall at any date and spot — essential for lining up sunsets with landmarks like Santorini's domes, Mount Fuji from Lake Kawaguchiko, or the Taj Mahal from Mehtab Bagh.

title: Monsoon-season travel: what it actually means
text: Monsoon rarely means all-day rain — in most of South and Southeast Asia it's an intense afternoon burst with clear mornings, lush landscapes, and 30–50% lower prices. Bali's wet season (November–March) still averages 5+ hours of sun a day. The real risks are washed-out mountain roads (avoid Himalayan road trips July–August), rough seas canceling island boats, and leech-season trekking. Pack a dry bag, quick-dry layers, and sandals you can wade in; book ground-floor rooms away from rivers.
